name: E2E: the write-time hook (in-repo rules + bundle + routed composite tools) blocks a violating write and allows a conformant write
command:
printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
  containers:
  - securityContext:
      privileged: true
' > pod.yaml && printf '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"%s/pod.yaml"}}' "$PWD" | bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/scripts/enforce-standards-on-save.sh" >/dev/null 2>blk.txt; bec=$?; printf 'name: app
version: 1.0.0
' > clean.yaml && printf '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"%s/clean.yaml"}}' "$PWD" | bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/scripts/enforce-standards-on-save.sh" >/dev/null 2>/dev/null; cec=$?; [ "$bec" -eq 2 ] && [ "$cec" -eq 0 ] && echo e2e5-write-time-enforced 1>&2
